criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking trade allocations as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (68.4% profitable allocation rate vs. 43.7% expected) and Chao's personal account (81.6% profitable allocation rate), quantifies the excess profits ($8,238,000 total, with $7,800,000 to Oceanic Fund I LP and $438,000 to Chao's personal account), and identifies the responsible parties (Chao as Head of Trading, Whitmore as facilitator). The allegation is treated as a standalone category with supporting evidence, key figures, and legal analysis.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not cite Sections 206(1) or 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation (Allegation Category 1). In the Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section, the agent only cites Rule 10b-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Sections 206(1) and 206(2) are mentioned in the output, but only in the context of the Breach of Fiduciary Duty / Directed Brokerage Arrangement section (Allegation Category 5), not in connection with the cherry-picking allegation.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation.
